name: The Aerodrome
description: Run a pioneer airline in the golden age of flight, where every route is a wager against weather, rivals and gravity.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "The Aerodrome". The stats are Command, Fuel & Funds, Crew, Navigation.

deck.name: The Aerodrome: Wings on a Shoestring
deck.description: Run a threadbare 1930s airline through monsoon skies, poaching rivals, and a government contract that will either lift you to glory or bury you in the mud.

cards.prompt: A rain-soaked postmaster slides a night airmail contract across his desk. 'Fly it in the dark and I'll pay double,' he says. 'Fly it late and I'll ruin you.'

rightChoice.text: Keep to daylight routes

cards.prompt: A wall of black cloud swallows your western route. Schedules are collapsing and the postmaster is watching the clock.

rightChoice.text: Ground the fleet and refund the mail

cards.prompt: A cigarette firm offers to sponsor a coast-to-coast speed record in your name. Glory sells tickets — but records kill airmen.

rightChoice.text: Politely decline the stunt

cards.prompt: A rival airline dangles fat salaries at your best pilots on the tarmac. Two are already packing their kits.

leftChoice.text: Match the offer to keep them

rightChoice.text: Let them walk, hire greenhorns

cards.prompt: Your grizzled navigator taps the monsoon chart. 'That valley route is a coffin, boss. I've buried men who trusted it.'

rightChoice.text: Take the fast valley route

cards.prompt: The government airmail contract goes to bid. Every airline in the region is sharpening its pencil.

rightChoice.text: Bid safe and sustainable

cards.prompt: A ministry clerk hints the contract needs 'a little grease' to reach your desk before the deadline.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and go through channels

cards.prompt: A snowbound pass could cut three hours off the run — if the downdrafts don't cut your wings first.

rightChoice.text: Fly the long safe arc

cards.prompt: Your head mechanic wipes oil from his hands. 'I keep these crates flying on prayer and half-pay. Fix one of those.'

leftChoice.text: Grant the whole crew a raise

rightChoice.text: Deny it, promise better times

cards.prompt: An engine coughs and dies over farm country. The pilot's voice crackles for orders.

leftChoice.text: Set down in a pasture, save the crew

rightChoice.text: Nurse the wounded bird home

cards.prompt: Two pilots come to blows over which approach to fly a fogged aerodrome. The hangar goes silent, waiting for your ruling.

rightChoice.text: Back the daring young flyer

cards.prompt: The widow of a pilot lost in the hills stands in your office, holding his flight log. 'He gave you everything.'

rightChoice.text: Offer only the bare minimum

cards.prompt: An eccentric millionaire wants a private charter to a jungle airstrip that isn't on any chart. He pays in cash, up front.

rightChoice.text: Refuse; stick to the mail

cards.prompt: A lantern tips in the fuel shed and half your reserve goes up in flame. Smoke curls over the aerodrome at dawn.

leftChoice.text: Buy emergency fuel at any price

rightChoice.text: Ration what's left, cut routes

cards.prompt: The Aviation Board arrives unannounced to inspect your logbooks, licenses, and creaking airframes.

rightChoice.text: Quietly bribe the inspector

cards.prompt: The monsoon front rolls in early. Fly through it now and you keep the schedule; wait and you lose the day.

rightChoice.text: Wait it out on the ground

cards.prompt: A green pilot begs for the record attempt. 'Give me the chance my father never got.' The others watch to see if you gamble on youth.

rightChoice.text: Tell him to earn his hours first

cards.prompt: A meteorologist offers you exclusive access to his new pressure charts — for a fee. 'Guess the weather, and you bury your pilots.'

rightChoice.text: Trust your own weathered instincts

cards.prompt: The contract is on the table at last. Sign exclusively to the government and lose your freedom — or stay independent and keep flying your own way.

rightChoice.text: Stay independent

cards.prompt: Dawn breaks over the aerodrome. Engines cough, warm, and roar — your battered airline still owns the sky it fought for. The pilots salute as the mail lifts off on time.

leftChoice.text: Raise a toast to the crews

rightChoice.text: Walk out to the flight line one last time

name: The Corner Office
description: Claw your way to the top of a corporation where every ally is a rival waiting for you to slip.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "The Corner Office". The stats are Influence, Capital, Workforce, Market Intel.

deck.name: The Corner Office
deck.description: Newly promoted amid a hostile takeover, you must survive scheming boards, a whistleblower, a restless union, and a seductive merger without your influence, capital, workforce, or market intel collapsing.

cards.prompt: Your first day in the corner office. The takeover war rages, and board veteran Diane Voss offers you her faction's backing—for a price of loyalty.

rightChoice.text: Stay independent

cards.prompt: A nervous accountant slips you a folder: proof the CFO cooked the quarterly numbers. She begs you to protect her identity.

rightChoice.text: Bury the documents

cards.prompt: Rival firm Halcyon Industries invites you to a discreet dinner. They dangle a 'friendly merger' that would end the takeover threat overnight.

rightChoice.text: Reject them coldly

cards.prompt: The union chief marches in. Layoff rumors have workers ready to strike unless you guarantee their contracts.

rightChoice.text: Stall for time

cards.prompt: A journalist calls: someone leaked internal memos to the press. The story goes live in hours unless you feed them a better narrative.

rightChoice.text: Go silent, no comment

cards.prompt: Board treasurer warns you the takeover raider is quietly buying shares through shell firms. He suggests a poison-pill defense.

rightChoice.text: Track the buyers instead

cards.prompt: An anonymous tip reaches you: the raider's financing is shaky. If true, a short squeeze could ruin them—but the tip could be a trap.

leftChoice.text: Bet company capital on the squeeze

rightChoice.text: Verify quietly first

cards.prompt: The CFO, cornered by the whistleblower's evidence, offers to resign quietly if you cover his golden parachute.

rightChoice.text: Fire him for cause

cards.prompt: Two directors clash in the boardroom: one demands aggressive expansion, the other total austerity. Both look to you to break the tie.

rightChoice.text: Side with austerity

cards.prompt: Markets wobble as rumors of the takeover leak. Your stock swings wildly. Analysts await your public move.

rightChoice.text: Let the numbers speak

cards.prompt: A hedge fund offers to buy a large stake and vote with you against the raider—if you give them a board seat.

rightChoice.text: Decline the outsider

cards.prompt: The whistleblower resurfaces: she's being followed and fears for her job. She asks you to make her situation public before it's too late.

rightChoice.text: Relocate her quietly

cards.prompt: Fallout from the whistleblower saga: regulators open an inquiry. Your legal team wants a strategy before subpoenas fly.

rightChoice.text: Lawyer up and stonewall

cards.prompt: Voss reappears—furious or grateful, depending on your choices—and calls a snap vote to decide the company's leadership.

rightChoice.text: Offer a power-sharing truce

cards.prompt: A middle manager begs for resources to keep a flagship project alive; killing it would save cash but crush morale.

rightChoice.text: Cut it loose

cards.prompt: Halcyon returns with a final ultimatum: merge now on their terms, or they'll join forces with the raider against you.

rightChoice.text: Negotiate a merger of equals

cards.prompt: A late-night press leak threatens to expose your own maneuvering. You can pin it on a rival or take responsibility.

rightChoice.text: Own it publicly

cards.prompt: The decisive shareholder meeting looms. The union, the board factions, and Halcyon all wait on your closing move.

rightChoice.text: Deliver shareholder value above all

cards.prompt: The votes are counted. The takeover is repelled, and you stand at the head of the table—your choices etched into the company's future. The corner office is yours to keep.

rightChoice.text: Reform the company for the long haul

name: Sands of the Nile
description: Rule as Pharaoh while the river, the priesthood and the gods themselves test your dynasty.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Sands of the Nile". The stats are Divine Authority, Grain Stores, The People, Temple Lore.

deck.name: The Year the River Forsook Egypt
deck.description: As a young Pharaoh facing a failed Nile flood, you must balance grasping priests, foreign threats, and a starving people to see your kingdom survive.

cards.prompt: The Nile has not risen. The black silt that feeds Egypt lies dry and cracked. Your vizier kneels, awaiting your first command.

leftChoice.text: Open the granaries and ration at once.

rightChoice.text: Summon the priests of Amun to divine the gods' will.

cards.prompt: The High Priest of Amun demands a doubled tribute of gold and grain to appease the river god.

leftChoice.text: Grant the tribute; the gods must be fed.

rightChoice.text: Refuse — the people need that grain.

cards.prompt: The vizier whispers that the granary tallies were counted twice and the numbers do not match. Someone is stealing.

rightChoice.text: There is no time; focus on the flood.

cards.prompt: The double count reveals a shortfall — ten thousand measures of grain, vanished.

rightChoice.text: Blame the drought and move on.

cards.prompt: The priests preach in the markets that Pharaoh has angered the gods. Whispers of impiety spread.

leftChoice.text: Send soldiers to silence the preachers.

rightChoice.text: Make a public offering to show your devotion.

cards.prompt: Tomb robbers have breached the necropolis. The dead kings' gold now funds bandits in the desert.

leftChoice.text: Send the palace guard to hunt them.

rightChoice.text: Post rewards for their capture among the people.

cards.prompt: The chief granary overseer begs mercy, offering to name his accomplices in exchange for his life.

rightChoice.text: Make an example of him before the city.

cards.prompt: The temple crowds swell into a riot at the palace gates. The priests hold back — for now.

leftChoice.text: Address the mob from the balcony yourself.

rightChoice.text: Bar the gates and wait them out.

cards.prompt: A Hittite envoy arrives bearing a chest of gold — and a warning that his king's armies march near your northern forts.

leftChoice.text: Accept the gold and hear his terms.

rightChoice.text: Refuse the gift; Egypt bows to no foreign king.

cards.prompt: Your scouts corner the robbers in a moonlit tomb, but the passages are trapped and dark.

rightChoice.text: Seal the tomb and starve them out.

cards.prompt: Hunger and rumor have turned to open revolt in the lower city. Fires light the night.

leftChoice.text: Distribute emergency grain to quell the anger.

rightChoice.text: Crush the revolt with force.

cards.prompt: The Hittite envoy offers a royal marriage to seal peace — his princess for your court.

rightChoice.text: Demand tribute from Hatti instead, or war.

cards.prompt: A merchant and a priest both claim the last barge of grain from Nubia. Both appeal to you.

leftChoice.text: Award it to the temple to keep the gods content.

rightChoice.text: Award it to the merchant to feed the markets.

cards.prompt: Starving villagers from the delta reach the palace, holding out empty hands and dying children.

leftChoice.text: Open the last royal reserves to them.

rightChoice.text: Turn them away; the reserves are sacred.

cards.prompt: Your astronomer-priests claim the flood may return if a great rite is performed at the shrine of the river's source.

rightChoice.text: Dismiss it as priestly fantasy.

cards.prompt: An old scroll in the temple archive describes a hidden granary sealed by your grandfather for a year such as this.

leftChoice.text: Follow the scroll to the sealed vault.

rightChoice.text: The vault is a myth; waste no men on it.

cards.prompt: At the source shrine, the priests await your word to begin a rite that has never been recorded as failing — or succeeding.

leftChoice.text: Perform the rite and stake your name on it.

rightChoice.text: Order simpler offerings, taking no great risk.

cards.prompt: The Hittite king's patience thins. His army sits at your border where the harvest that never came should have arrived.

leftChoice.text: Meet him with your assembled army and envoys.

rightChoice.text: Send lavish gifts to buy another season of peace.

cards.prompt: Word comes at dawn: dark clouds gather over the southern mountains, and the river's edge glistens. All Egypt holds its breath.

leftChoice.text: Proclaim the flood's return in your name and lead the offerings.

rightChoice.text: Wait in silence until the waters are certain.

cards.prompt: The Nile rises. Silt-brown waters spill across the fields as crowds chant your name from the temples to the delta. You survived the year the river forsook Egypt.

leftChoice.text: Give thanks to the gods who spared your people.

rightChoice.text: Claim the miracle as proof of your divine reign.

name: House of Ravenmoor
description: Inherit a cursed estate whose debts are paid in secrets, and whose secrets are paid in blood.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "House of Ravenmoor". The stats are Dread, The Estate, The Village, The Occult.

deck.name: The House of Ravenmoor
deck.description: You inherit a cursed gothic estate where ghostly bargains, a resentful village, and a crypt that should have stayed shut test whether you rule by dread, gold, mercy, or forbidden knowledge.

cards.prompt: The solicitor lays the deed to Ravenmoor before you, his hand trembling. 'The family... conditions... are unusual,' he murmurs, avoiding your eyes.

leftChoice.text: Sign at once, curse and all.

rightChoice.text: Demand to read the fine print first.

cards.prompt: Before he leaves, the solicitor grips your sleeve. 'The west wing is sealed for a reason. Whatever you do, do not break the seals.'

leftChoice.text: Heed him and leave it sealed.

rightChoice.text: Resolve to explore it tonight.

cards.prompt: A delegation of villagers assembles at the iron gates, demanding to know if the old cruelties of Ravenmoor will continue under you.

rightChoice.text: Forgive their debts to win goodwill.

cards.prompt: Beneath the chapel floor, an iron crypt door groans against its chains. Something within scratches politely, as if requesting entry to the world above.

rightChoice.text: Brick it up and pretend it isn't there.

cards.prompt: At the stroke of midnight a pale stranger knocks, dressed in a fashion two centuries out of date. 'A guest of the old master,' he says with a smile too wide.

rightChoice.text: Bar the door and salt the threshold.

cards.prompt: A weeping specter in the gallery offers a bargain: her peace in exchange for a promise to right an old wrong done to the village.

rightChoice.text: Bargain instead for the gold she guards.

cards.prompt: Behind a false wall you discover the Ravenmoor hoard: coin, plate, and jewels wrenched from the village over generations.

leftChoice.text: Claim it all for the estate.

rightChoice.text: Return the plunder to the villagers.

cards.prompt: Your housekeeper and the village elder come to blows over grazing rights on the moor. Both look to you to settle it.

rightChoice.text: Uphold the estate's ancient claim.

cards.prompt: In the library sits a tome bound in something that is not leather, its pages warm to the touch and whispering when the candle gutters.

rightChoice.text: Cast it into the hearth.

cards.prompt: The family curse stirs: mirrors weep, the portraits age, and your own reflection lags a heartbeat behind you.

leftChoice.text: Fight the curse with prayer and iron.

rightChoice.text: Embrace the curse and its power.

cards.prompt: A travelling medium offers to hold a séance in the great hall, promising answers — for a price, and with no guarantees.

leftChoice.text: Fund the séance and take part.

rightChoice.text: Expose her as a fraud and send her off.

cards.prompt: A disinherited cousin arrives, claiming the true right to Ravenmoor and hinting he knows how to lift — or worsen — the curse.

rightChoice.text: Draw him into your schemes.

cards.prompt: The village priest arrives with censer and holy water, demanding to cleanse Ravenmoor before its evil spills into his flock.

rightChoice.text: Cast the priest from your land.

cards.prompt: The seals on the west wing have weakened. Cold air pours from the gap, and a voice that sounds like your own name leaks through.

leftChoice.text: Reinforce the seals and walk away.

rightChoice.text: Fling the wing open at last.

cards.prompt: A ghostly benefactor, once master of Ravenmoor, offers endless wealth if you will only continue his 'traditions' beneath the crypt.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and banish his shade.

cards.prompt: Torches gather at the gates. The village, terrified by strange lights in the east wing, has come to burn Ravenmoor to its foundations.

leftChoice.text: Let the east wing burn to appease them.

rightChoice.text: Face the mob with cold authority.

cards.prompt: An ailing villager, marked by the curse, begs you for release — either mercy, or a place in whatever dark rite might save the rest.

rightChoice.text: Bind their fading soul to your work.

cards.prompt: In the deepest vault you find the curse's root: a bargain struck by the first Ravenmoor, still binding, still hungry, awaiting an heir's decision.

rightChoice.text: Seize its power for yourself.

cards.prompt: Everything converges in the crypt at midnight: the village at your back, the ghosts at your side, the book open in your hands. One last choice remains.

leftChoice.text: Break the curse and free Ravenmoor.

rightChoice.text: Bind the curse to your will as its master.

cards.prompt: Dawn breaks over Ravenmoor. The house still stands, changed — and so are you. Whether feared, revered, or merely endured, you are its master now, and the crypt is quiet at last.

leftChoice.text: Rule as the enlightened lord of Ravenmoor.

rightChoice.text: Take your throne among its immortal dead.

name: The Uneasy Crown
description: Hold a medieval throne through famine, feuding lords and a war nobody can afford.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "The Uneasy Crown". The stats are The Crown, The Coffers, The Subjects, The Council.

deck.name: The Uneasy Crown
deck.description: Two winters into a reign born of contested succession, you must survive thin harvests, restless barons, a demanding crusade, and the whispers of advisors who each want your ear and your ruin.

cards.prompt: The Baron of the Marches has not renewed his oath since your coronation. His riders now collect tolls on the king's roads as though the crown were a rumor.

leftChoice.text: Muster the levies and march north

rightChoice.text: Summon him to court to negotiate

cards.prompt: The march bogs down in autumn mud. Half-starved conscripts begin slipping away into the night.

leftChoice.text: Press on and hang the deserters

rightChoice.text: Withdraw and pardon the men

cards.prompt: The Baron rides into court smiling, and asks that his lands be freed of all crown taxes in exchange for his renewed loyalty.

rightChoice.text: Refuse gold, offer him an empty title

cards.prompt: The harvest comes in thin and blighted. The granaries will not last the winter for both court and country.

leftChoice.text: Open the royal granaries to the people

rightChoice.text: Ration tightly and hoard the reserve

cards.prompt: A delegation of village elders kneels in the great hall, begging relief from the winter tithes.

rightChoice.text: Promise aid come spring and send them home

cards.prompt: Word arrives that hunger has become fury; bakers' stalls burn in the capital's lower wards.

leftChoice.text: Send the city guard to restore order

rightChoice.text: Distribute grain in the squares at once

cards.prompt: Your spymaster slides a ledger across the table. 'The Chancellor skims from every levy,' she whispers. 'I can prove it—or make it disappear for the right coin.'

rightChoice.text: Trust the Chancellor, dismiss her claim

cards.prompt: The riots harden into open rebellion; a mob raises a banner bearing your rival's arms.

rightChoice.text: Meet the rebel leaders to bargain

cards.prompt: The audit is damning: the Chancellor's ledgers are forged, and gold has vanished into his private vaults.

leftChoice.text: Arrest him publicly and seize his estate

rightChoice.text: Blackmail him into silent obedience

cards.prompt: The treasury clerk faints at his desk: the Chancellor has fled in the night with the winter's taxes.

leftChoice.text: Send riders to run him down

rightChoice.text: Bury the scandal to save face

cards.prompt: The rebellion is broken, but the countryside smolders and refugees clog the roads to the capital.

leftChoice.text: Proclaim amnesty for those who return home

rightChoice.text: Garrison the region with iron discipline

cards.prompt: A crusade envoy in gilded mail demands levies of men and gold for the holy war in the east.

leftChoice.text: Pledge troops and treasure to the cause

rightChoice.text: Refuse; your realm cannot spare a coin

cards.prompt: The Archbishop arrives, cold with displeasure, questioning your piety before the assembled court.

leftChoice.text: Fund a great cathedral to appease the Church

rightChoice.text: Remind the Church that the crown rules here

cards.prompt: Your crusaders win glory beneath foreign walls, but every dispatch begs for more men and coin.

leftChoice.text: Recall the levies to tend the harvest

rightChoice.text: Let them press on to greater triumph

cards.prompt: Two barons come to blows over a border river, and each swears the other diverts the water to steal his fields. Both await your judgement.

leftChoice.text: Rule for the baron who kept his oath

rightChoice.text: Split the river and satisfy neither

cards.prompt: Your victorious general returns to cheering crowds. He is beloved now—perhaps too beloved—and lingers near the throne.

leftChoice.text: Honor him richly and pension him off

rightChoice.text: Arrange a quiet, permanent retirement

cards.prompt: The spymaster and the new Chancellor now feud openly in council, each demanding you choose whose word to trust.

rightChoice.text: Back the Chancellor's steady hand

cards.prompt: A neighboring king offers his daughter's hand, and with her an alliance that would silence the doubters of your claim forever.

rightChoice.text: Decline; your throne needs no foreign prop

cards.prompt: The rival claimant raises a banner one last time, gathering the disaffected to challenge your right by force.

leftChoice.text: Meet them upon the field of battle

rightChoice.text: Offer them honor and a seat at your court

cards.prompt: The third winter of your reign settles quiet over the realm. The barons kneel, the granaries hold, and the crown at last sits without trembling upon your brow. You have made a kingdom of the ruin you inherited.

leftChoice.text: Rule now in peace, having earned it

rightChoice.text: Set your gaze upon greater ambitions

name: Night Shift
description: Keep a city hospital alive through an outbreak, a budget crisis and a board that smells liability.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Night Shift". The stats are Authority, Funding, Patients & Staff, Research.

deck.name: Night Shift: The Ashford General Crisis
deck.description: As the new medical director of a broke city hospital facing a novel infection, every swipe trades authority, funding, staff trust, and hard science against the clock.

cards.prompt: Your first night. The ER overflows with a coughing, feverish crowd nobody can name yet. The board chair calls: 'Keep it quiet until we understand it.' A resident begs you to declare a public alert.

rightChoice.text: Obey the board, say nothing yet

cards.prompt: Supplies are thin. Your head nurse shows you a stockroom with three days of masks left. A vendor offers an emergency shipment at triple price, cash tonight.

rightChoice.text: Ration and refuse

cards.prompt: A journalist films patients on gurneys in the hallway and posts it. The board demands you deny everything. The public wants the truth.

rightChoice.text: Issue a flat denial

cards.prompt: Your funds took a hit, but the ward is stocked. A pharma rep appears offering free doses of an experimental antiviral in exchange for using your patients as a trial site.

rightChoice.text: Refuse, protect patients

cards.prompt: Rationing sparked fear. Two nurses collapsed from exhaustion and exposure. The union rep warns a strike vote is coming unless conditions improve.

leftChoice.text: Promise hazard pay you can't afford

rightChoice.text: Invoke emergency mandatory shifts

cards.prompt: The infection has a name now: a fast-spreading respiratory strain. The city health office asks you to share your patient data for a regional model.

rightChoice.text: Withhold to protect the hospital

cards.prompt: Early trial numbers look promising but the sample is tiny. A doctor wants to give the antiviral to every ER patient tonight. Your ethicist objects.

rightChoice.text: Wait for more evidence

cards.prompt: Without the trial money, payroll is tight. A wealthy donor offers a large gift—if you name the new ward after his failed pharmaceutical company.

leftChoice.text: Take the donation and the name

rightChoice.text: Decline the vanity deal

cards.prompt: The strike vote passed threshold; nurses will walk at dawn unless you act. The board says fire the ringleaders. Your COO says negotiate.

rightChoice.text: Sit down and negotiate

cards.prompt: A patient given the experimental antiviral suffers a severe reaction and dies. The family threatens to go to the press. Legal wants a settlement and a gag order.

leftChoice.text: Settle quietly with a gag order

rightChoice.text: Admit the risk publicly, pause the drug

cards.prompt: Caution has slowed you. A rival hospital publishes the treatment protocol you were too careful to try, taking the credit and the grant money.

rightChoice.text: Focus purely on patient care

cards.prompt: Whatever you chose, the ward is stretched. A retired physician volunteers to lead a triage overflow tent in the parking lot, unpaid.

rightChoice.text: Refuse—liability too high

cards.prompt: The board convenes an emergency meeting. They're split: half want you gone for the bad press, half credit you for keeping doors open.

leftChoice.text: Present your data and stand firm

rightChoice.text: Offer to share power with a co-director

cards.prompt: A national broadcaster wants a live tour of your hospital tonight—a chance to attract funding, or a disaster if things look chaotic.

rightChoice.text: Decline, keep control of the message

cards.prompt: Federal aid finally arrives: ventilators, funds, and inspectors. The inspectors want to review every decision you've made so far.

rightChoice.text: Cooperate but shield past mistakes

cards.prompt: Peak night. The overflow tent floods with patients and rain. Your staff, exhausted, look to you for one clear order.

leftChoice.text: Convert the closed east wing overnight

rightChoice.text: Divert new arrivals to other hospitals

cards.prompt: Your refined treatment data is now solid enough to publish. A journal offers rapid release, but a co-author demands their name lead the paper.

leftChoice.text: Let them lead to speed publication

rightChoice.text: Insist on shared, honest credit

cards.prompt: The infection curve is finally bending. The board wants a triumphant statement claiming they planned it all. The staff want recognition.

rightChoice.text: Let the board take the spotlight

cards.prompt: Dawn after the worst week. One last decision: a permanent pandemic-preparedness unit is proposed, funded by cutting elsewhere. Your legacy hangs on it.

rightChoice.text: Bank the savings for stability

cards.prompt: The surge has broken. Ashford General still stands—understaffed, scarred, but open. Whether they call you a reformer or a survivor, the lights stayed on and the doors never closed. The night shift is over.

rightChoice.text: Hand the keys to the day shift

name: The War Room
description: Command a border war where the maps are wrong, the supplies are late and the politicians are watching.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "The War Room". The stats are Command, Supplies, Morale, Intelligence.

deck.name: Hold the Winter Line
deck.description: Given a faltering border command, you must juggle enemy night raids, a scheming rival officer, closing mountain passes, and a headquarters hungry for a newspaper victory.

cards.prompt: You arrive at the crumbling border camp. The men are exhausted, the pickets thin, and Colonel Vance eyes you like a rival. How do you take command?

rightChoice.text: Tour the lines quietly first

cards.prompt: A scout reports the enemy probes your wire every single night, testing for weakness.

rightChoice.text: Conserve strength, ignore the probes

cards.prompt: A supply wagon is ambushed on the mountain road and abandoned in a ravine.

leftChoice.text: Send a patrol to recover it

rightChoice.text: Write off the loss

cards.prompt: Your quartermaster begs for a rationing plan before the stores run dry.

rightChoice.text: Keep full rations for morale

cards.prompt: Colonel Vance openly countermands your standing orders in front of the staff.

rightChoice.text: Let it slide to keep the peace

cards.prompt: A patrol returns with a captured enemy map showing troop dispositions.

leftChoice.text: Trust it and plan around it

rightChoice.text: Suspect a trap and verify it

cards.prompt: A night raid tears through your camp, torches in the dark.

rightChoice.text: Fall back to the inner line

cards.prompt: Two captains blame each other for a failed sortie and demand you judge them.

rightChoice.text: Side with the sharp young officer

cards.prompt: Headquarters wires: they need a victory for the morning papers, and they need it now.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and demand more time

cards.prompt: Deserters are dragged back in chains as whispers of mutiny spread.

rightChoice.text: Execute them for discipline

cards.prompt: You could stage a risky dawn assault purely to give HQ its headline.

rightChoice.text: Cancel and hold ground

cards.prompt: Snow is falling; within days the mountain passes will be sealed shut.

rightChoice.text: Fortify for a winter siege

cards.prompt: Mutiny erupts in the ranks; a company refuses to man the wire.

rightChoice.text: Negotiate with the ringleaders

cards.prompt: HQ offers reinforcements, but only if you accept their handpicked staff.

rightChoice.text: Decline and stay independent

cards.prompt: The field medic pleads for supplies to save the wounded before the frost takes them.

rightChoice.text: Reserve supplies for the fight

cards.prompt: An enemy envoy rides in under a white flag, proposing a local winter truce.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and probe their lines

cards.prompt: A narrow pass offers a chance to flank the enemy before it closes with snow.

rightChoice.text: Play it safe with a frontal feint

cards.prompt: Final preparations. The whole campaign now hinges on your last decision.

rightChoice.text: Dig in and let them break on you

cards.prompt: The assault begins at dawn, the valley echoing with gunfire.

rightChoice.text: Command from the rear

cards.prompt: The guns fall silent. The line held through the winter, and the passes are sealed behind a beaten enemy. The papers have their victory, and your battered command survives to see the thaw.

rightChoice.text: Return quietly to your troops

name: Olympus Falling
description: Rise as a young god in a pantheon where worship is currency and prophecy is a loaded weapon.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Olympus Falling". The stats are Divinity, Tribute, Mortals, Prophecy.

deck.name: Olympus Falling: The Latecomer's Gambit
deck.description: A newly ascended god scrambles for a throne in a crumbling pantheon while a chained titan wakes and the Fates keep smiling.

cards.prompt: A gaunt village priest kneels in your first shrine, offering to raise a temple in your name if you will answer his prayers.

rightChoice.text: Demand a grander shrine

cards.prompt: The elder gods summon you to the high hall and command that you kneel and swear deference before dining at their table.

rightChoice.text: Refuse to kneel

cards.prompt: The three Fates smile at you over their loom, weaving a thread the color of your name and saying nothing at all.

rightChoice.text: Turn your back on them

cards.prompt: Deep beneath the mountain, the chained titan shifts and the peaks tremble. The old bindings are fraying.

rightChoice.text: Ignore it, court worship instead

cards.prompt: A wealthy city offers its collective worship if you will grant them one great and public miracle.

rightChoice.text: Demand tribute before any wonder

cards.prompt: You lean over the well of prophecy, its waters churning with futures that could shatter or crown you.

rightChoice.text: Sip only a careful glimpse

cards.prompt: Another newly ascended deity, hungry as you, proposes an alliance against the elders' contempt.

rightChoice.text: Spurn the upstart

cards.prompt: Your first temple is complete and a great festival roars in your honor. The crowd waits to see how a young god behaves.

rightChoice.text: Collect the heaped tribute

cards.prompt: A cruel drought withers the fields and desperate mortals crawl to your altars begging for rain.

rightChoice.text: Withhold rain until they pay

cards.prompt: The elder council calls a vote on whether to grant you a lesser seat at the divine table.

rightChoice.text: Sway them with fiery oratory

cards.prompt: A plague spreads through the very cities that chant your name, and priests die at their altars.

leftChoice.text: Burn your divinity to heal them

rightChoice.text: Study the dying for prophecy

cards.prompt: The merchant princes offer a river of gold if you become the patron of their trade and turn a blind eye to their sins.

rightChoice.text: Refuse their tainted gold

cards.prompt: An oracle whispers that the titan's chains will snap on the next dark moon, and that only a god who acts first may profit.

rightChoice.text: Hoard the secret for leverage

cards.prompt: A war-general prostrates himself before your idol, begging for victory in the coming battle.

rightChoice.text: Demand a blood sacrifice first

cards.prompt: An elder god's forgotten relic lies unguarded — a font of raw power that could vault you above your station, if you dare thieve from your betters.

rightChoice.text: Leave it untouched

cards.prompt: The chains finally shatter. The titan claws upward and the elder gods, cowardly, look to you to see what a hungry newcomer will do.

rightChoice.text: Seek prophecy of its weakness

cards.prompt: Two great cities go to war, each swearing they alone are your truest worshippers, each demanding you smite the other.

rightChoice.text: Force a peace between them

cards.prompt: You stand upon the trembling mountain as the titan rises to its full and terrible height. The moment of your legend arrives.

rightChoice.text: Ensnare it with prophecy's trick

cards.prompt: With the titan subdued, the pantheon offers you a throne — a subordinate one, unless you take a greater seat by force.

rightChoice.text: Seize a greater seat by force

cards.prompt: The elders bow, the mortals chant your name, and the Fates smile one last knowing smile as your thread is woven gold into the heart of Olympus. You have your seat — for now.

rightChoice.text: Rule with an iron fist

name: Dead Reckoning
description: Captain a merchant vessel through storms, mutiny and a war that keeps redrawing the shipping lanes.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Dead Reckoning". The stats are Captaincy, Cargo, The Crew, The Charts.

deck.name: Dead Reckoning: The Merchant's Gamble
deck.description: Command a merchant sailing ship through naval war, storm seasons, press gangs, and a syndicate's sharp lawyers while balancing your authority, your cargo, your restless crew, and the truth of your charts.

cards.prompt: Dawn at the quay. War has thinned the sea lanes and doubled the risk. A slow navy convoy forms at the harbor mouth, or you can slip out alone on the morning tide.

rightChoice.text: Sail alone and fast

cards.prompt: Your first mate presents a tallied list of grievances: short rations, unpaid shares, a leaky forecastle. He waits, arms folded, for your answer before the men.

rightChoice.text: Dismiss him sharply

cards.prompt: An early squall of the storm season swallows the horizon. The barometer plunges. Ride it through under reefed sail, or heave to and shelter in a lee.

rightChoice.text: Shelter and wait

cards.prompt: A stranded factor offers extra bales of dyed cloth at a bargain, if you'll cram them into an already-full hold. The crew eyes the crowded deck warily.

rightChoice.text: Decline, keep her trim

cards.prompt: Your grizzled navigator taps the chart. 'These shoals aren't where the Admiralty prints them, Captain. I've sounded them myself.' Trust his weather-craft or the printed line?

rightChoice.text: Trust the printed chart

cards.prompt: A king's cutter comes alongside. A press-gang officer demands able seamen for the fleet. Your best topmen shrink behind the mast.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and stall them

cards.prompt: Beyond the marked banks lies an uncharted channel that could shave three days off the passage. It is not on any map you carry.

rightChoice.text: Keep to known water

cards.prompt: The bosun begs an extra rum ration to steady frayed nerves after the storm. Grant it and win smiles, or hold the cask and hold the line.

rightChoice.text: Keep the cask locked

cards.prompt: A raked black sail crests the swell astern — a privateer hunting stragglers. She has the weather gauge and gains fast.

rightChoice.text: Crack on and flee

cards.prompt: In a quiet cove, a shore-runner offers to load untaxed brandy for a fat commission — no questions, no manifest, no syndicate's cut.

rightChoice.text: Refuse the contraband

cards.prompt: The shortcut betrays you — the keel grinds on an unmarked bar and the hull groans. The tide is falling fast.

leftChoice.text: Work all hands to kedge off

rightChoice.text: Jettison cargo to float free

cards.prompt: Two crewmen come to blows over salvaged plunder — a gunner and the helmsman, each swearing the other lies. The deck falls silent for your verdict.

rightChoice.text: Side with the helmsman

cards.prompt: A syndicate lawyer waits in a dispatch boat, waving papers. The insurers demand a full cargo survey before they'll honor your policy. Comply, or bluff that all is in order?

rightChoice.text: Bluff the lawyer off

cards.prompt: A wandering chart-maker in a port tavern offers freshly corrected coastal charts and tide tables — dear, but signed by a master pilot.

rightChoice.text: Trust your own reckoning

cards.prompt: Fever spreads below decks; two men are down and more feel poorly. Divert to a fever-port for a surgeon, or press on and hope it breaks.

rightChoice.text: Press on regardless

cards.prompt: The first mate returns, hat in hand but eyes hard, asking you formally pledge the crew a larger share of the voyage's profit.

rightChoice.text: Refuse the demand

cards.prompt: The final crossing of the season looms, gales stacking on the horizon. Run before the wind for speed, or claw a careful, longer track home?

rightChoice.text: Navigate careful and slow

cards.prompt: A wartime blockade rings the home approaches. A revenue captain hints a 'gratuity' would speed your papers, or you could slip in through the fog banks by night.

rightChoice.text: Sneak through the fog

cards.prompt: The home harbor opens ahead — but the syndicate's clerks are already tallying your losses against the policy. Settle their dues honestly, or contest every farthing in court?

rightChoice.text: Contest it in court

cards.prompt: Warps ashore, sails furled, the ship rides safe at her moorings. The crew is paid, the manifest closed, the charts marked with hard-won truth. You made port against the odds — the ledger and the loyalty are yours to weigh.

leftChoice.text: Toast the crew and the voyage

rightChoice.text: Begin planning the next passage

name: Under the Black Flag
description: Keep a pirate crew fed, feared and unhanged in a sea running out of easy prizes.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Under the Black Flag". The stats are Fear, Plunder, The Crew, Sea-Lore.

deck.name: Under the Black Flag
deck.description: An elected pirate captain must juggle a mutinous crew, a tightening navy net, a governor's poisoned pardon, a rival's fleet, and a torn treasure map on the way to a legendary wreck.

cards.prompt: The crew hauls you onto the barrel and cheers your election as captain. Their grins say the vote can go the other way just as fast. What do you swear before them?

rightChoice.text: 'Every course put to a vote.'

cards.prompt: A navy frigate crests the horizon, gunports yawning open. The wind is with them.

leftChoice.text: Come about and give them a broadside

rightChoice.text: Slip into the fogbank and vanish

cards.prompt: A skiff flying parley colors brings a governor's envoy. He speaks of a royal pardon 'for a captain of your talents.'

leftChoice.text: Hear the terms in your cabin

rightChoice.text: Run the black flag up and send him home

cards.prompt: The envoy lays it out: surrender your best guns, name your fellow captains, and kneel to the crown. The purse he slides across the table is heavy.

leftChoice.text: Take the purse and the strings

rightChoice.text: Haggle for a pardon without the informing

cards.prompt: Word of the pardon talk races through the lower deck. Knives are being whispered over. Some smell betrayal.

leftChoice.text: Call the crew and swear you'd never sell them

rightChoice.text: Flog the loudest doubter into silence

cards.prompt: Red Isabel, captain of the Carrion Rose, hails you. She proposes a joint fleet — twice the guns, twice the terror, split the seas between you.

leftChoice.text: Sign the fleet articles with her

rightChoice.text: Keep your ship your own

cards.prompt: The fleet's first squabble: Isabel demands your master gunner for her flagship, 'for the good of us all.'

rightChoice.text: Refuse and stare her down

cards.prompt: The fleet corners a fat merchant convoy under escort. Isabel signals for the plan.

rightChoice.text: Pick off the stragglers by night

cards.prompt: A lone merchantman wallows off the reef, low and heavy in the water. Easy prey — but her captain claims to know secret channels.

leftChoice.text: Take her hold and let her burn

rightChoice.text: Spare him for his charts and knowledge

cards.prompt: You unroll the fabled map to the wreck of the San Cristobal — and its lower corner, the corner bearing the final bearings, has been torn away.

leftChoice.text: Comb the port taverns for the missing corner

rightChoice.text: Sail on the bearings you can guess

cards.prompt: A one-eyed cartographer produces the torn corner from a locked box. 'Everything has a price, Captain.'

rightChoice.text: Put a pistol to his good eye

cards.prompt: Your quartermaster reads the sky and the reassembled chart. 'A hurricane sits square across the wreck's waters, Captain.'

leftChoice.text: Anchor in the lee and wait it out

rightChoice.text: Ride the storm's skirts and press on

cards.prompt: The navy has strung a blockade across the only strait to the wreck. The net is closing.

leftChoice.text: Run the guns at full sail

rightChoice.text: Grease a harbor official to look away

cards.prompt: The crew splits: grizzled veterans want the cautious deepwater route, the young bloods want the fast reef passage. Both look to you.

rightChoice.text: Let the young bloods run the reef

cards.prompt: The wreck's waters churn. A great whirlpool guards the graveyard of ships, spinning slow and hungry.

leftChoice.text: Read the currents and thread the eye

rightChoice.text: Crowd on sail and bull straight through

cards.prompt: The San Cristobal glimmers below, her gold spilling from a split hull. The dive is deadly and dark.

leftChoice.text: Send divers deep and fast for the lot

rightChoice.text: Rig careful salvage over days

cards.prompt: As the treasure comes aboard, sails appear — Red Isabel and a navy sloop both, each meaning to claim your prize.

leftChoice.text: Clear for action and fight them off

rightChoice.text: Parley and buy them off with a cut

cards.prompt: Back in open water, the crew calls the vote that always comes after a great haul: does the captain divide fair?

rightChoice.text: Take the captain's double cut

cards.prompt: The governor's pardon still waits, sealed and valid. You could die rich and forgiven ashore — or die a legend beneath the black flag.

leftChoice.text: Take the pardon and swallow the anchor

rightChoice.text: Refuse it and sail into the horizon

cards.prompt: Whichever wind you chose, the ballads are already being sung in a hundred ports. Your name is spoken with a lowered voice and a raised cup — the captain who kept the black flag flying and lived to answer for it.

leftChoice.text: Raise a final toast to the crew

rightChoice.text: Set the course and say nothing

name: After the Ash
description: Lead a settlement in the ruins, where old-world tech is treasure and every stranger is a verdict.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "After the Ash". The stats are Force, Scrap & Stores, Settlers, Old-World Tech.

deck.name: After the Ash: The Hollow Wall
deck.description: A generation after the collapse, you rule a walled settlement squeezed between circling raiders, a dwindling water table, a spy-riddled caravan, and a humming pre-collapse bunker that promises salvation or ruin.

cards.prompt: Dawn watch signals smoke on the eastern ridge. The gate captain wants orders before the settlers wake and panic.

leftChoice.text: Send light scouts to learn the truth

rightChoice.text: Reinforce the walls and wait

cards.prompt: Your scouts return running. A raider band is probing the west wall, testing the gate hinges by night.

leftChoice.text: Repel them with a hard sortie

rightChoice.text: Hold the wall and shout for parley

cards.prompt: A dust-caked caravan reaches your gate, wagons heavy with medicine and merchants with too-watchful eyes.

rightChoice.text: Turn them away; distrust their eyes

cards.prompt: The old well-keeper drops a mud-stained bucket at your feet. The water table is falling faster each season.

rightChoice.text: Dig a deeper shaft with salvaged pumps

cards.prompt: Beneath the collapsed hospital, diggers uncover a sealed hatch. A low hum vibrates through the stone—a pre-collapse bunker, still powered.

rightChoice.text: Weld it shut; some doors stay closed

cards.prompt: A scarred envoy from the Ash-Wolf clan rides in under a bone banner, demanding tribute for 'safe passage.'

rightChoice.text: Defy them from the wall

cards.prompt: A farming family begs to leave for rumored greener valleys, and others are watching to see if you let them go.

leftChoice.text: Let them leave with a share of stores

rightChoice.text: Persuade them to stay and rebuild

cards.prompt: The main well coughs up mud and stops. Buckets come up dry and the settlement holds its breath.

rightChoice.text: Raid the neighbor clan's spring

cards.prompt: The caravan's medicine is limited. Mothers and militia both crowd the store-house door, and words turn to shoving.

leftChoice.text: Give it to the sick children first

rightChoice.text: Reserve it for the fighters who guard all

cards.prompt: The bunker's blast door is jammed. A rusted keypad blinks beside it, and your engineer chews her lip.

leftChoice.text: Pry it open with jacks and muscle

rightChoice.text: Take days to crack the code cleanly

cards.prompt: Inside, screens flicker awake. A calm synthetic voice greets you as 'Administrator' and offers to help manage the settlement.

rightChoice.text: Cut its power; trust no ghost

cards.prompt: A caravan hand is caught mapping the bunker's corridors, a coded ledger sewn into his coat.

leftChoice.text: Execute the spy as a warning

rightChoice.text: Turn him; buy his secrets with mercy

cards.prompt: A coughing sickness spreads through the crowded lower quarter, faster than the medicine can reach.

rightChoice.text: Run the bunker's medical bay at full power

cards.prompt: Scavengers stumble on a buried fuel cache beneath a toppled tanker—still sealed, still precious.

leftChoice.text: Hoard the fuel for the militia

rightChoice.text: Share it to warm every hearth

cards.prompt: Riders from the Greenhollow settlement propose an alliance: their spears for your bunker's secrets.

leftChoice.text: Accept the alliance and its price

rightChoice.text: Stay independent behind your wall

cards.prompt: Your engineer confides that those who spend long hours with the bunker's screens grow distant, murmuring in numbers.

leftChoice.text: Heed her; ration the tech's use

rightChoice.text: Push deeper; knowledge is survival

cards.prompt: The bunker houses a dormant automated turret grid. The command console waits, cursor blinking.

leftChoice.text: Fire it on the massing raiders

rightChoice.text: Keep it armed as silent deterrent

cards.prompt: The scattered raider clans have united under one warlord. Torches ring your walls at dusk—a true siege begins.

leftChoice.text: Sally out to break their line

rightChoice.text: Seal the gates and outlast them

cards.prompt: The last night before the assault. You climb the wall to face your people, who wait for a reason to hold.

leftChoice.text: Inspire them with words and shared fire

rightChoice.text: Trust the old-world machines to win it

cards.prompt: Dawn breaks over broken siege-ladders. The warlord's banner burns, the wells run clear from the deep pumps, and children play in the ash-gardens. Your settlement endures into a new generation—still walled, still watchful, but alive.

leftChoice.text: Carve the fallen's names into the gate

rightChoice.text: Turn your eyes to the horizon and rebuild

name: First Fire
description: Guide a tribe through the long winter of the world, when fire is new and the herds decide who eats.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "First Fire". The stats are Strength, Food Stores, The Tribe, Fire-Wisdom.

deck.name: First Fire: Keepers of the Ember
deck.description: At the edge of the endless ice, you lead a tribe that guards a jealous flame while herds shift, rivals circle, and a stranger brings a new way to shape stone.

cards.prompt: The fire pit gutters as an ice-wind claws down from the glacier. If it dies, the long dark takes us all.

leftChoice.text: Shield the flame with your own bodies

rightChoice.text: Feed it the last of the driftwood

cards.prompt: A gaunt stranger walks out of the white, carrying blades knapped finer than any you know.

leftChoice.text: Welcome him and learn his craft

rightChoice.text: Send him back into the cold

cards.prompt: The shaman crouches over the fire, reading twisting smoke. 'The omens say the herds have turned,' she rasps.

leftChoice.text: Heed her and break camp early

rightChoice.text: Trust the old grounds and stay

cards.prompt: Scouts return breathless: the great herds no longer pass the northern valley. The tracks lead far away.

leftChoice.text: Chase the herds across the barrens

rightChoice.text: Hold near the safe camp

cards.prompt: A rival band shadows your hunters, watching every kill from the ridgelines.

rightChoice.text: Quietly yield the far grounds

cards.prompt: A young hunter, hungry for glory, begs to lead his own party into the ice.

rightChoice.text: Keep him at your side

cards.prompt: A toothless elder woman kneels by the coals, asking for a double share to survive the freeze.

rightChoice.text: Ration must be equal for all

cards.prompt: The old fire-keeper collapses, fever-bright. Only she remembers how to coax flame from wet tinder.

rightChoice.text: Name a new keeper at once

cards.prompt: In her last lucid nights, the keeper offers to whisper the deepest secrets of the ember.

leftChoice.text: Learn every rite of the flame

rightChoice.text: Let the old ways die with her

cards.prompt: A great tusked beast wallows in a frozen ravine — meat for a whole moon, if it can be taken.

rightChoice.text: Trap it with fire and pits

cards.prompt: Two hunters both claim the killing blow — and thus the hide and honor.

leftChoice.text: Award it to the seasoned elder

rightChoice.text: Split the kill evenly between them

cards.prompt: A wide-eyed child tugs your furs, pleading to be taught how to make fire himself.

rightChoice.text: He is too young for the flame

cards.prompt: A blizzard swallows the world. Snow buries the camp and the fire chokes on ice-fog.

leftChoice.text: Huddle close and burn the stores

rightChoice.text: Send hunters out in the storm

cards.prompt: Beyond a wall of ice you find a deep, wind-sheltered cave, warm with the earth's breath.

rightChoice.text: Keep it as a hunting outpost

cards.prompt: The rival chieftain offers peace, sealed by joining a daughter of his blood to your fire.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and stand alone

cards.prompt: The shaman insists the flame is angry and demands a great offering fed into its mouth.

leftChoice.text: Feed precious stores to the fire

rightChoice.text: Refuse the wasteful rite

cards.prompt: With the stranger's teaching, you could reshape all the tribe's tools — but it will cost hunting days.

rightChoice.text: Hunt now, learn later

cards.prompt: Fresh grounds lie across a groaning sheet of sea ice that cracks underfoot.

leftChoice.text: Cross while the way is open

rightChoice.text: Wait for the ice to thicken

cards.prompt: A final storm lashes the camp and the great fire shrinks to a single trembling ember.

leftChoice.text: Cradle the ember with your own body

rightChoice.text: Rally the whole tribe to wall the wind

cards.prompt: Dawn breaks clear. The fire roars tall, the tribe gathers around it, and the stranger's blades gleam in hands that now know their craft. You have carried the first fire through the long dark, and it will outlive you.

leftChoice.text: Name a new keeper and rest at last

rightChoice.text: Lead them onward toward the greening lands

name: The Way of the Blade
description: Serve as a daimyo's right hand in a province where honor and survival keep drawing different maps.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "The Way of the Blade". The stats are Authority, Koku, The Clan, Bushido.

deck.name: The Way of the Blade
deck.description: As chief retainer to a young daimyo, you must weather a border insult, a short rice tax, gathering ronin, and an early shogunate inspector without shattering your clan.

cards.prompt: Lord Kanmori's banners fly a mocking device above the river ford — a deliberate slur against your young daimyo. The war-drums of the province wait on your answer.

leftChoice.text: Demand a duel to answer the insult

rightChoice.text: Send a measured letter of protest

cards.prompt: A hot-blooded young samurai kneels before you, begging leave to cross blades with Kanmori's champion and wash away the shame himself.

leftChoice.text: Restrain the hothead, keep him alive

rightChoice.text: Let the young samurai duel

cards.prompt: In a lantern-lit tea house you sit across from Kanmori's silk-tongued envoy. The kettle sings while both of you measure the other's patience.

leftChoice.text: Ply the envoy with gifts and sake

rightChoice.text: Project cold strength across the table

cards.prompt: The tax ledgers are grim: the rice levy comes up badly short after a poor season. The storehouses will not fill themselves.

leftChoice.text: Raise the levy on the villages

rightChoice.text: Forgive part of the tax this year

cards.prompt: Masterless men — ronin — gather in the pine hills above the pass, robbing pilgrims and eyeing the harvest. Their captain sends word he is for hire.

rightChoice.text: Hunt them down before harvest

cards.prompt: A courier gallops in mud-spattered: the shogunate's inspector will arrive within days, weeks ahead of schedule, to weigh your lord's fitness to rule.

rightChoice.text: Receive him with honest austerity

cards.prompt: A rice merchant of Sakai offers coin against next year's crop — enough to fill the granaries now, if you will bend your pride to a townsman's terms.

rightChoice.text: Refuse; the clan will not beg

cards.prompt: The gray-bearded elders of the council convene, murmuring that a boy-lord and his over-bold karo court ruin. They wish to guide your hand.

rightChoice.text: Assert your own command

cards.prompt: Your captains spread a map by the brazier: strike Kanmori's undefended border forts while the snows pin his main army. A winter campaign, bold and cruel.

rightChoice.text: Delay until the spring thaw

cards.prompt: A scribe in your lord's household is unmasked as Kanmori's ear — his brushes carry your secrets over the border.

rightChoice.text: Turn the spy to your service

cards.prompt: Gaunt peasants crowd the castle gate, bowing to the frozen earth, begging for the granary's mercy before winter takes their children.

leftChoice.text: Open the granaries to the people

rightChoice.text: Ration strictly, feed the soldiers

cards.prompt: Kanmori, wary of the inspector's eye, proposes peace sealed by his daughter's marriage to your daimyo — and a generous dowry of rice-land.

rightChoice.text: Reject it; defiance over dowry

cards.prompt: By torchlight the hill ronin descend on the granary, blades bared. The choice is battle or bargain, and the snow is already red.

rightChoice.text: Buy them off with silver

cards.prompt: The inspector's report will shape whether the shogunate keeps your lord or replaces him. His clerk hints, delicately, that gold speaks louder than virtue.

leftChoice.text: Bribe the inspector for a favorable report

rightChoice.text: Accept the shogun's judgment with grace

cards.prompt: Alone in the keep, your young daimyo confesses he fears he is a puppet — that the clan follows you, not him. His eyes search yours for the truth.

rightChoice.text: Seize the regency in all but name

cards.prompt: The army bogs down in a whiteout on the mountain road. Provisions dwindle, men frostbite in the ranks, and the far fort still stands unbroken.

rightChoice.text: Retreat and spare the men

cards.prompt: Kanmori's champion strides to the field and calls the clan out by name. The inspector watches from his palanquin. Honor demands a blade answer.

rightChoice.text: Send your finest champion

cards.prompt: The war narrows to a single snow-choked pass. Hold it and the province is saved; lose it and Kanmori waters his horses in your fields.

leftChoice.text: Hold the line to the last

rightChoice.text: Parley for terms

cards.prompt: Word reaches you that a senior vassal has been dealing with the enemy behind your back — the rot is inside your own hall.

rightChoice.text: Forgive, and bind the clan together

cards.prompt: The snows melt. The inspector's palanquin departs with a report, Kanmori's banners withdraw, and the young daimyo — grown taller in a hard winter — turns to his karo for the shape of the peace.

leftChoice.text: Broker a lasting peace and secure the young lord's rule

rightChoice.text: Claim the province's ascendance in your lord's name

name: Critical Mass
description: Direct a research institute racing toward a breakthrough that funders, rivals and generals all want first.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Critical Mass". The stats are Influence, Grants, Public Trust, Discovery.

deck.name: Critical Mass: The Discovery Ledger
deck.description: You steer a research institute toward an industry-shattering breakthrough while funding cliffs, ambitious stars, snooping rivals, and defense contractors test how much truth you can afford.

cards.prompt: The endowment runs dry in ninety days, yet the lab is tantalizingly close. Accountants and scientists shout past each other in the hallway.

rightChoice.text: Spend it all chasing the breakthrough

cards.prompt: Dr. Vane, your star researcher, wants to publish the preliminary results now, before anyone else can claim them.

rightChoice.text: Insist she wait for verification

cards.prompt: A rival lab announces work eerily close to yours, phrased with details only your team should know.

rightChoice.text: Ignore them and outrun the competition

cards.prompt: A defense contractor calls for the fourth time this week, hinting at 'flexible, generous, discreet' funding.

rightChoice.text: Decline politely

cards.prompt: The ethics board demands to review your data handling before any further trials proceed.

rightChoice.text: Override them citing urgency

cards.prompt: A replication attempt fails. The signal that made everyone giddy may be noise.

rightChoice.text: Bury the doubt and press on

cards.prompt: At 3 a.m. the instruments spike with the result you've dreamed of. Nobody else has seen it yet.

rightChoice.text: Announce the triumph immediately

cards.prompt: The renewal grant arrives with strings: quarterly deliverables and a seat for the funder on your steering committee.

rightChoice.text: Seek independent funders instead

cards.prompt: The leak hunt narrows to two suspects: a homesick postdoc and a suspiciously chatty administrator.

leftChoice.text: Publicly accuse the rival lab of theft

rightChoice.text: Run a discreet internal audit

cards.prompt: A science journalist wants an exclusive on the 'lab that could rewrite an industry.'

rightChoice.text: Hype the story to draw investors

cards.prompt: A junior researcher warns she was pressured to fudge a calibration to hit the announced timeline.

leftChoice.text: Back her and reopen the data

rightChoice.text: Dismiss it as cold feet

cards.prompt: You can run one decisive, expensive experiment that will either confirm the breakthrough or end it.

leftChoice.text: Go all in on the big test

rightChoice.text: Take a cheaper, partial approach

cards.prompt: The board of directors, nervous about headlines, demands you slow down and protect the institute's brand.

rightChoice.text: Defy them for science

cards.prompt: Your old mentor visits and gently asks whether you're chasing truth or chasing the win.

rightChoice.text: Trust your own ambition

cards.prompt: The defense contractor lays a lucrative contract on the table, demanding exclusive rights and a nondisclosure clause.

rightChoice.text: Walk away to keep the work open

cards.prompt: The findings are solid enough to publish. How you release them will define the institute for a decade.

rightChoice.text: Rush the flashy paper to beat the rival

cards.prompt: An independent group replicates your result. The phones will not stop ringing.

rightChoice.text: Rush to patent and monetize first

cards.prompt: A final review panel will decide whether the discovery stands as legitimate science.

rightChoice.text: Request more time to bulletproof it

cards.prompt: Cash, credibility, and morale all wobble at once. The institute teeters on collapse.

leftChoice.text: Accept emergency funding on any terms

rightChoice.text: Rally the staff to see it through

cards.prompt: The panel confirms it: your institute has rewritten the industry, on its own terms. History will remember how you got here.

leftChoice.text: Dedicate the discovery to open science

rightChoice.text: Secure the institute's legacy and future

name: The Season
description: Manage a storied club through a make-or-break season of injuries, egos and boardroom knives.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "The Season". The stats are Leverage, Payroll, The Fans, Scouting.

deck.name: Dead Club Walking
deck.description: Manage a storied, fading football club through one make-or-break season where every contract, headline, and boardroom betrayal decides whether you and the club survive.

cards.prompt: The owner slides a dossier across the mahogany: relegation form, empty seats, and your contract's final clause. 'Save my club, or I'll find someone who can.'

leftChoice.text: Promise a title challenge and spend like you mean it.

rightChoice.text: Pitch a patient, youth-led rebuild instead.

cards.prompt: Your talismanic striker's contract expires in weeks. His agent smiles: 'Record wages, or he tests the market.'

leftChoice.text: Let him walk and bank the wages.

rightChoice.text: Cave and hand him the golden deal.

cards.prompt: Deep in the academy, a scout waves a folder of underage gems. Investing now could bear fruit—eventually.

rightChoice.text: Sell the best kids for quick cash.

cards.prompt: The owner forwards a marketing email: he wants a 'shirt-selling superstar,' not a spreadsheet signing.

rightChoice.text: Overrule him with a data-driven bargain.

cards.prompt: A rival club circles your best defender with an offer, and his head is turned.

leftChoice.text: Match their ambition to keep him.

rightChoice.text: Cash in before his value drops.

cards.prompt: The press box has already published your obituary: 'Dead Club Walking.' Cameras wait for a reaction.

leftChoice.text: Dismiss the press; trust your process.

rightChoice.text: Feed them a bold headline promise.

cards.prompt: Ultras unfurl a banner during training: 'AMBITION OR RESIGNATION.' Stewards look to you.

leftChoice.text: Meet the supporters and hear them out.

rightChoice.text: Have security clear them out.

cards.prompt: The academy prodigy begs for a first-team chance. The coaches whisper he's a season too raw.

leftChoice.text: Throw him into the starting XI now.

rightChoice.text: Loan him out to season him quietly.

cards.prompt: An emergency audit reveals the wage bill is drowning the club. The finance director looks pale.

rightChoice.text: Gamble on staying the course.

cards.prompt: Derby day. Win and the season lives; lose and the vultures feast. Attack or absorb?

leftChoice.text: Grind out a gritty, ugly win.

rightChoice.text: Go all-out attacking spectacle.

cards.prompt: A tabloid exposes your star's agent brokering shady deals. The story is spreading fast.

leftChoice.text: Cut ties publicly and take the loss.

rightChoice.text: Bury it with a quiet settlement.

cards.prompt: An injury crisis guts your squad three games from safety. The physio needs a call.

rightChoice.text: Blood the kids and protect the veterans.

cards.prompt: Two senior players brawl in the dressing room; each demands the other be dropped.

rightChoice.text: Side with the disciplined professional.

cards.prompt: A tech firm offers sponsorship—if you let their analysts embed in your scouting room.

rightChoice.text: Refuse and guard your independence.

cards.prompt: Deadline day, minutes left. A wonderkid is available on loan—or a proven veteran for a fee.

rightChoice.text: Pay up for the safe veteran.

cards.prompt: The supporters' trust calls a confidence vote in your management live on radio.

leftChoice.text: Assert your authority and stand firm.

rightChoice.text: Promise concessions and share power.

cards.prompt: A boardroom faction moves to strip your transfer control before the run-in.

leftChoice.text: Fight the coup with your allies.

rightChoice.text: Concede power for job security.

cards.prompt: The final match that decides everything looms. The squad watches how you carry yourself.

leftChoice.text: Trust the squad; project calm belief.

rightChoice.text: Drill them relentlessly, no mercy.

cards.prompt: At the pre-final presser, a reporter offers you the narrative—hero or scapegoat—if you'll play along.

rightChoice.text: Stay guarded and let results talk.

cards.prompt: The final whistle blows on your season. Whatever the scoreline, the club's fate—and yours—is sealed by the choices that carried you here.

leftChoice.text: Walk the touchline one last time, head held high.

rightChoice.text: Sign the new contract they slide across the desk.

name: Steampunk Chronicles
description: Command a clockwork army in a world powered by steam and ingenuity. Will your inventions save the empire or cause its downfall?
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Steampunk Chronicles". The stats are Empire's Favor, Aetherium Cells, Public Opinion, Forbidden Blueprints.

deck.name: The Perpetual Engine: A Brasspunk Chronicle
deck.description: As the empire's chief artificer you juggle a war-hungry crown, a hoarding inventors' guild, saboteurs in the foundries, and a forbidden blueprint that promises an engine which never stops.

cards.prompt: The Iron Crown's herald clanks into your workshop: 'His Majesty demands a fleet of war engines within the month, Chief Artificer.'

leftChoice.text: Kneel and pledge the engines will fly.

rightChoice.text: Warn that the aetherium reserves are too thin.

cards.prompt: The Inventors' Guild has locked its aetherium vaults, letting cells gather dust while the foundries starve.

rightChoice.text: Send guards to seize the vaults by royal decree.

cards.prompt: A saboteur has slit the pressure lines in Foundry Three; scalding steam floods the gantries.

leftChoice.text: Lead the repair crews yourself through the vents.

rightChoice.text: Seal the foundry and let the shift burn out.

cards.prompt: A cloaked courier slides you a blueprint stamped FORBIDDEN: an engine that never stops turning.

rightChoice.text: Burn it and report the courier to the Crown.

cards.prompt: A soot-faced apprentice kneels: 'Master, my sister's ward needs one aetherium cell to keep breathing.'

leftChoice.text: Give her a cell from your stores.

rightChoice.text: Refuse; every cell is spoken for by the Crown.

cards.prompt: The perpetual mechanism you sketched hums on the bench, but its gears glow an impossible violet.

leftChoice.text: Cool it with rationed cells and keep testing.

rightChoice.text: Push the output higher to impress the Crown.

cards.prompt: An airship captain begs for finished engines before the enemy fleet crosses the mountains.

leftChoice.text: Prioritize her warships over palace parades.

rightChoice.text: Serve the palace first; parades keep the Crown pleased.

cards.prompt: The Guild offers a truce: they'll share aetherium if you teach them the forbidden design.

leftChoice.text: Share only the safe, incomplete schematics.

rightChoice.text: Hand them the full blueprint for a fortune in cells.

cards.prompt: Dockside smugglers offer crates of unlicensed aetherium, no questions, no papers.

leftChoice.text: Buy the crates and hide the ledgers.

rightChoice.text: Turn them away to keep your hands clean.

cards.prompt: Foundry widows crowd your gate, demanding pensions for men lost to the engines.

leftChoice.text: Fund the pensions from the war budget.

rightChoice.text: Promise medals instead of coin.

cards.prompt: Your steam-clerk whispers that the Guild plans a coup once they master your engine.

leftChoice.text: Feed them a sabotaged version of the design.

rightChoice.text: Ignore the rumor and trust the truce.

cards.prompt: The never-stopping engine is ready for its first true ignition beneath the palace.

rightChoice.text: Open it full; let the Crown witness a miracle.

cards.prompt: Two foundry chiefs come to blows over who commands the aetherium line.

leftChoice.text: Back the veteran who keeps quotas high.

rightChoice.text: Promote the reformer the workers adore.

cards.prompt: A Crown inquisitor demands to know where the forbidden blueprint came from.

leftChoice.text: Name the courier and surrender a decoy sketch.

rightChoice.text: Plead ignorance and shred every record.

cards.prompt: Overdriven, the engine ruptures a wing of the foundry in a blast of violet fire.

leftChoice.text: Take the blame publicly and vow repairs.

rightChoice.text: Blame the Guild's inferior cells.

cards.prompt: A defected Guild engineer warns the perpetual core will not stop even if you want it to.

leftChoice.text: Design a fail-safe governor in secret.

rightChoice.text: Dismiss the warning; the Crown loves it too much.

cards.prompt: The Guild, humiliated, threatens to strike unless you return their reserves.

leftChoice.text: Return a share and keep the peace.

rightChoice.text: Break the strike with royal marines.

cards.prompt: The Crown wants the engine mass-produced; the blueprint's final secret still eludes you.

rightChoice.text: Gamble on decoding the last forbidden page.

cards.prompt: Alone in the dark, you face the last page of the forbidden blueprint.

leftChoice.text: Complete the perpetual engine, consequences be damned.

rightChoice.text: Burn the final page and settle for what works.

cards.prompt: The great engines rise over the city of brass, never stopping, and history records the artificer who lit their hearts.

leftChoice.text: Stand before the Crown and the roaring crowd.

rightChoice.text: Slip away into the steam, your legacy secured.

name: Fathom Station
description: Command a deep-sea station where the pressure outside is nothing next to the pressure within.
systemInstruction: You are a creative storyteller for the interactive fiction game "SwipeVerse". You create challenging scenarios themed as "Fathom Station". The stats are Command, Air & Supplies, The Crew, The Deep.

deck.name: Fathom Station: The Circling Light
deck.description: Miles beneath the surface, you command a starving mining station while a shape circles the lights and the trench itself whispers through your instruments.

cards.prompt: The resupply sub is six days overdue. Oxygen reserves are thinning, and the crew keeps glancing at the airlock that stays empty. Corporate hasn't answered your last three pings.

leftChoice.text: Cut air ration and stretch supplies

rightChoice.text: Break protocol and hail the corporate uplink

cards.prompt: With rations cut, the recycler techs report they can scrub more air if you divert power from the perimeter lights. But the lights are the only thing keeping the sonar contact at a distance.

leftChoice.text: Dim the lights to save air

rightChoice.text: Keep the lights burning bright

cards.prompt: Corporate answers at last: hit the mineral quota this cycle or the sub 'reprioritizes' to a more profitable site. They weigh your air against their margins.

leftChoice.text: Push the crew to meet the quota

rightChoice.text: Refuse and log a safety hold

cards.prompt: Your chief engineer corners you in the dark corridor. 'We're breathing recycled fear down here. Give me twelve hours off the drills and I'll fix the scrubbers for good.'

rightChoice.text: Order drilling to continue

cards.prompt: The sonar contact tightens its orbit, brushing the outer floodlights. It moves against the current, deliberate. The night watch swears it paused to look inside.

leftChoice.text: Ping it aggressively with active sonar

rightChoice.text: Go silent and observe

cards.prompt: A survey drone maps a mineral vein richer than anything on the manifest. Corporate would kill for it — but it lies beneath the trench edge where the instruments always fail.

rightChoice.text: Flag it for study instead

cards.prompt: Every gauge on the trench-facing sensors drifts in unison, as if pulled by an unseen tide. The science officer wants to shut them down and recalibrate; it will cost you air and time.

rightChoice.text: Trust the drift and log it

cards.prompt: A young technician requests emergency evacuation on the next sub — if it ever comes. 'I can hear it at night, Commander. It's calling the pressure hull.' Others are listening to her.

leftChoice.text: Promise her a seat and calm the deck

rightChoice.text: Confine her to quarters for morale

cards.prompt: You send the ROV over the trench lip to scout the vein. Its feed floods with static — then, for one frame, something vast and geometric, before the tether goes taut and screaming.

leftChoice.text: Reel it in fast, whatever the cost

rightChoice.text: Let it dive deeper for one more frame

cards.prompt: A seam weeps seawater along Bay Three. Minor now, but the trench pressure is patient. Sealing it means welding through the crew's rest cycle.

leftChoice.text: Seal it immediately, crew be damned

rightChoice.text: Monitor and patch at shift change

cards.prompt: The contact stops circling. It holds station a hundred meters out, facing the viewport, matched to the station's every drift. The lights flicker in a rhythm that is not the generator's.

leftChoice.text: Signal back in the same rhythm

rightChoice.text: Kill all external emissions and hide

cards.prompt: The station doctor reports three crew with pressure-sickness tremors — or something like it. She needs the med bay's power and your authority to quarantine them.

rightChoice.text: Keep them working; you can't spare hands

cards.prompt: Sonar finds a wrecked survey pod snagged on a ledge, its supply cache possibly intact. Reaching it means sending divers into the contact's old orbit.

leftChoice.text: Send the dive team for the cache

rightChoice.text: Leave the dead to the deep

cards.prompt: The science officer decodes the light-rhythm: it maps the trench's magnetic pulse. 'It isn't hunting us,' she whispers. 'It's teaching us where the interference comes from.'

leftChoice.text: Follow the mapped path to the source

rightChoice.text: Seal the data and protect the crew

cards.prompt: You order a manned dive to the trench floor to end the mystery. The pressure suits can survive it — probably. The crew waits on your word, sweat fogging their visors.

rightChoice.text: Send a volunteer team with limits

cards.prompt: The mining crew and science team come to blows in the mess: one side wants to strip the vein and buy passage home, the other wants to study the anomaly. Both look to you.

leftChoice.text: Side with the miners and their quota

rightChoice.text: Side with the scientists and the deep
